description_long: This traffic barricade is constructed primarily from a solid, light gray material, likely concrete or a composite, and features a horizontal plank supported by two A-frame legs on either end. The horizontal plank is adorned with diagonal orange and white striping, serving as a high-visibility warning pattern. The A-frame legs provide stability and are joined to the plank with visible cross-bracing, giving the structure a robust and sturdy appearance. The surface appears rough and matte, with the striping painted or adhered onto the main body. There are no visible lights, signage, or attachment fixtures, and the object is designed to be freestanding. Its utilitarian design is typical for use in roadworks, construction zones, or to block access in urban environments.
description: A sturdy, gray A-frame traffic barricade with diagonal orange and white stripes across the horizontal plank, designed for high visibility and stability.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the barricade with prominent diagonal orange and white striping on the horizontal plank.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the A-frame support structure and the thickness of the horizontal plank.
description_view_2: Rear view, visually similar to the front, displaying the same striped plank and A-frame supports.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again highlighting the A-frame structure and the plank's thickness.
attribute_reflective_striping_pattern: Diagonal orange and white stripes, medium width, repeating across the horizontal plank.
